resources.string: Follow these steps to move your ZeroRelay account to a new phone. Account backup does not include local chat history; you will need to exchange new messages after migrating.
In Settings, export an account backup (.zrab) with a passphrase of at least 8 characters. The file includes identity, contacts, groups, ratchet state, relay URL, and TLS pins.
Move the .zrab file and passphrase via USB, encrypted cloud storage, or in-person AirDrop. Do not send the backup or passphrase through chat apps.
After installing ZeroRelay, import the .zrab file from Settings using the same passphrase. Import replaces all local account data.
In Settings, save and test the connection. If the relay TLS certificate rotated, trust the new pin when prompted before chatting.
Spot-check contacts and send a test message
Confirm contact and group counts look right, then send a test message to one contact to verify send/receive works.
If contacts show as unverified, compare safety numbers in person and mark them verified before sending sensitive content.
Confirm each item below to finish your migration.
Connection test failed — check URL and network
After migrating, compare safety numbers in person again
Return home, open a contact chat, and send a message to confirm delivery works.
The relay certificate changed — trust the new TLS pin before continuing.
Backup exported — store the .zrab file and passphrase securely; do not send them via chat apps
